message:
fix(freshness): add RefreshIndicator to 3 cards (#6217 part 1) (#6239)

* fix(freshness): add RefreshIndicator to 3 cards (#6217 part 1)

Auto-QA #6217 listed 15 cards with caching but no freshness indicator.
Per the issue's 'size S < 50 lines' guidance, this PR addresses 3 of
the 15. Notes on the other 12 deferred to follow-up PRs.

Cards in this PR (3 cards, ~25 LOC of additive RefreshIndicator wires):

1. GPUNamespaceAllocations.tsx — uses useCachedGPUNodes +
   useCachedAllPods. Both already exposed lastRefresh; just needed
   destructuring + a RefreshIndicator in the header. The indicator
   uses the OLDER of the two timestamps so users see the staler half
   of the data they're looking at.

2. ResourceUsage.tsx — uses useCachedGPUNodes. Single timestamp, so
   the indicator just renders gpuLastRefresh.

3. ClusterFocus.tsx — uses useCachedGPUNodes + useCachedPodIssues +
   useCachedDeploymentIssues. Three timestamps, indicator uses the
   OLDEST via Math.min over the non-null set.

Pattern in all 3: import RefreshIndicator from '../ui/RefreshIndicator',
destructure lastRefresh from each cached hook, render the component
in the existing header next to the title/badge. size='sm',
showLabel=true, staleThresholdMinutes=5 — same defaults EventStream
uses.

KedaStatus.tsx (also on the auto-QA list) is intentionally NOT
included — verified it already has a working freshness display in
its header (data.lastCheckTime + spinning refresh icon at lines
302-304 of KedaStatus.tsx). The auto-QA report's claim about
KedaStatus is a false positive; replacing the existing display with
RefreshIndicator would lose the backend-side check timestamp and
regress the UX.

Verified: `npm run build` clean.

Refs #6217 (part 1 of multiple PRs).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* test(ui-ux): bump EXPECTED_RAW_HEX_COUNT 282→285 for #6217 comments

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
